memberdef.argsstring: (rc_stage_t type, void *ptr, const float _Complex *in, size_t n_in, float _Complex *out, size_t max_out)
memberdef.name: _stage_exec

briefdescription.para: Create a rate converter for the given output/input rate ratio.

parameterdescription.para: Output-to-input sample rate ratio. Any positive float.
Non-zero to append a CIC passband-droop compensating FIR after any CIC stage.

simplesect.para: Non-NULL on success; NULL if rate <= 0 or OOM.

briefdescription.para: Free all resources. NULL is a no-op.

detaileddescription.para: Rate and stage structure are preserved. Processing from a reset state produces the same output as a freshly created converter.

memberdef.argsstring: (RateConverter_state_t *s, const float _Complex *in, size_t n_in, float _Complex *out, size_t max_out)
memberdef.name: RateConverter_execute

briefdescription.para: Convert n_in samples and write results to out.
Upper bound on execute output for a standard 65536-sample block.

detaileddescription.para: Returns (size_t)(65536 * max(rate, 1.0)) + 2. The Python extension uses this to pre-allocate the output buffer on the first execute call.

briefdescription.para: Change the rate; rebuilds the cascade and resets all filter state.
Write a human-readable label for stage i into buf.

simplesect.para: 1 on success, 0 if i is out of range.

memberdef.argsstring: (double rate, int compensate, const float _Complex *in, size_t n_in, float _Complex *out, size_t max_out)
memberdef.name: RateConverter_convert

briefdescription.para: One-shot rate conversion — no persistent state required.

detaileddescription.para: Use RateConverter_create() directly when processing multiple blocks at the same rate — the one-shot form resets filter memory on every call.

parameterdescription.para: Non-zero to enable CIC droop compensation.

simplesect.para: Number of output samples written; 0 only if OOM or n_in == 0.

detaileddescription.para: Stage selection mirrors _plan_cascade() from the validated Python prototype (src/doppler/resample/rate_converter.py, 33/33 tests).
The CIC "compound stage" bundles the CIC decimator and its optional droop-compensating FIR into a single cascade slot so that n_stages exactly equals the number of human-readable stage labels.
Intermediate ping-pong buffers (bufs[0] and bufs[1]) are grown lazily on the first execute call. buf_cap equals n_in so any decimation stage's intermediate output fits without a second alloc.
